what is the maximum transfer speed of cat 5e

The maximum transfer speed of Cat 5e cable is 1 gigabit per second (Gbps). However, it is important to note that the actual transfer speed may vary depending on several factors such as cable length, signal interference, and network equipment.

The maximum transfer speed of Cat 5 cable is also 1000 Mbps or 1 Gbps. However, it has been largely superseded by the newer Cat 5e and Cat 6 cables which have better specifications and more advanced features.

The maximum transfer speed of Cat 4 cable is 10 to 16 megabits per second (Mbps), which is much slower than the newer and more advanced standards such as Cat 5, Cat 5e, and Cat 6. Cat 4 cable was commonly used in the 1990s, but it has been largely replaced by newer and faster technologies.
when did bass boosters come around?

Bass boosters or bass amplifiers have been around for many decades. The first commercial bass guitar amplifier, the Ampeg Portaflex B-15, was introduced in 1960 and had a built-in bass boost control. This was a groundbreaking product, as it allowed bass players to boost their low frequencies and produce a more powerful sound. Since then, bass boosters have become a standard feature in many types of audio equipment, including headphones, car stereos, and home theater systems. The exact year when bass boosters became a standard feature in consumer audio equipment is unclear, but it is believed to be during the 1970s and 1980s.
When did car subwoofers become popular?

Car subwoofers became popular in the 1980s and 1990s. At that time, car audio systems started to become more advanced and people began to add subwoofers to their car audio systems to get a higher level of bass performance. The introduction of the first Carver M-400t amplifier in the 1980s made it possible for car audio enthusiasts to power larger subwoofer systems, which helped to increase their popularity. Subwoofers are now a standard part of many car audio systems, and the market for car subwoofers has continued to grow and evolve with advancements in technology.
What was the first subwoofer made for the car?

The first subwoofer designed specifically for use in a car was the Infinity Servo-Statik 1, introduced in 1968. It was developed by engineer and inventor, James B. Lansing, who founded the Lansing Manufacturing Company (JBL) in 1946. The Servo-Statik 1 was a 12-inch long-throw driver based on JBL's professional loudspeaker technology, and it was designed to reproduce low frequencies more accurately and with more power than other car audio systems of that time. While the Servo-Statik 1 was the first subwoofer developed for use in a car, it was a high-end product and not widely adopted due to its high price. It was not until the 1980s and 1990s that the use of car subwoofers became more widespread as the technology became more affordable and accessible.
where is the world's largest ikea

The world's largest IKEA store is located in South Korea. The store is called the IKEA Gwangmyeong store and measures approximately 640,000 square feet (59,000 square meters). It opened in December 2014 and features a showroom, market hall, restaurant, children's play area, and over 50 room settings. The IKEA Gwangmyeong store is also notable for its sustainable design, featuring a rooftop solar panel system that provides approximately 10% of the store's total electricity needs.
